cases.title: Testing-plan §11 NFR-03 verification row — valid check-in processing success rate at or above 99%
preconditions: Testing-plan §7.1 performance tests and §11 NFR verification matrix row for NFR-03 apply.
Representative pilot load fixture is seeded: multiple open class sessions with enrolled students, valid rotating QR tokens, and satisfied authentication and policy preconditions.
Test harness can classify each POST /v1/check-ins attempt as rule-pass (valid) or rule-fail (intentional rejection) and aggregate success rate for the valid subset only.
steps: Execute a representative campus load run where enrolled students submit rule-pass check-in requests (open session, valid token, authenticated, enrolled, policy satisfied).
For each rule-pass request, record terminal outcome: HTTP 200 with data.outcome Success and persisted AttendanceRecord, or processing failure (5xx, timeout, or Success not persisted).
Compute success rate as successful rule-pass requests divided by total rule-pass requests in the run.
Exclude intentional rule-fail attempts (ExpiredQr, NotEnrolled, DuplicateCheckIn, SessionClosed, etc.) from the valid-request denominator per NFR-03 definition.
cases.expected: Valid check-in processing success rate is >= 99% across the pilot load run, satisfying NFR-03 and linked AC-22 gate per testing-plan §7.1 and §11 minimum evidence.

cases.title: Check-in service atomically persists Success outcome and attendance record for valid requests
preconditions: Real database transaction boundary is enabled for check-in service, CheckInAttempt, and AttendanceRecord modules.
Open class session with valid QR token exists; authenticated enrolled student has no prior success for the session.
Effective policy preconditions (GPS if required) are satisfied for the test student.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, Idempotency-Key, and student bearer token.
Verify CheckInAttempt row is inserted with outcome Success and correlation ID.
Verify AttendanceRecord row is created or updated to Present or Late in the same transaction boundary.
Repeat for a statistically meaningful sample of distinct valid requests under normal integration conditions.
cases.expected: Each rule-pass check-in command atomically persists Success attempt and attendance ledger row; sample success rate across valid integration requests is 100%, contributing to the >= 99% NFR-03 gate under aggregated load.

cases.title: Valid check-ins succeed only when session is Open; closed-session rejections excluded from success-rate denominator
preconditions: Class session exists in Scheduled state with enrolled students and assigned lecturer.
Lecturer bearer token is available for POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open and /close.
Valid QR tokens are obtainable while session is Open.
steps: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer; verify state Open.
Submit rule-pass POST /v1/check-ins from enrolled students with valid tokens; record Success outcomes.
POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close; verify state Closed.
Submit additional check-in with previously valid token after close; verify 422 SessionClosed rejection.
Aggregate success rate using only rule-pass requests submitted while session was Open.
cases.expected: All rule-pass requests during Open state achieve Success; post-close attempts are classified as rule-fail (SessionClosed) and excluded from valid-request denominator. Open-window valid success rate remains >= 99% per NFR-03.

cases.title: Class-start concurrent burst on POST /v1/check-ins maintains >= 99% valid processing success
preconditions: Multiple open class sessions each have 20+ enrolled students per representative pilot profile.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests across sessions with distinct students, valid QR tokens, and unique Idempotency-Key values.
All requests satisfy session-open, token, enrollment, authentication, and policy preconditions per NFR-03 valid-request subset.
Retry storm subset replays same Idempotency-Key for already-accepted commands per testing-plan §7.2.
steps: Trigger simultaneous rule-pass check-in submissions across concurrent open sessions mimicking class-start burst (performance-smoke profile).
Include idempotent replays: resubmit with same Idempotency-Key after Success; verify idempotent success response.
Include duplicate distinct submissions after Success; verify 409 DuplicateCheckIn classified as rule-fail, not processing failure.
Compute success rate for rule-pass first-attempt and idempotent-replay requests only.
cases.expected: Under class-start concurrency and retry storms, valid check-in processing success rate is >= 99% (NFR-03); idempotent replays return Success without duplicate attendance rows; intentional DuplicateCheckIn rejections are excluded from the valid-request denominator.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins returns HTTP 200 Success envelope for >= 99% of rule-pass requests under load
preconditions: Open class sessions with valid qrTokens and enrolled students are seeded at pilot scale.
E2E harness can dispatch bulk rule-pass POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization, Idempotency-Key, and valid payloads.
Harness records HTTP status, envelope shape (data/meta/error), and data.outcome per request.
steps: Dispatch N rule-pass POST /v1/check-ins requests (N >= 1000 for statistical confidence) across representative open sessions.
For each response, classify as processing success (HTTP 200, data.outcome Success, data.attendanceStatus Present or Late) or processing failure (5xx, timeout, or 200 without persisted success).
Compute success rate = processing successes / N.
Verify failed rule-pass responses use structured error envelope when applicable, not silent drops.
cases.expected: HTTP 200 success envelope with data.outcome Success is returned for >= 99% of rule-pass requests; success rate meets NFR-03 gate and API-G01 deterministic check-in under peak load target.

cases.title: WF-03 primary student check-in happy path achieves >= 99% processing success under expected load
preconditions: Representative enrolled class sections with pilot-scale student counts are configured.
Lecturer has opened sessions with rotating valid QR tokens.
Student actors are enrolled, can authenticate, and satisfy policy preconditions for check-in.
steps: For each representative section, enrolled students execute WF-03 happy path: obtain valid token, authenticate if needed, submit POST /v1/check-ins.
Classify each submission as rule-pass (eligible valid request) or rule-fail (intentional rejection scenario).
For rule-pass submissions only, record whether processing completed with Success and queryable Present or Late attendance.
Aggregate success rate across all rule-pass WF-03 attempts in the load run.
cases.expected: Primary WF-03 happy-path processing success rate for rule-pass requests is >= 99%, matching NFR-03 measurable objective and AC-22 acceptance gate.

cases.title: Processing success gate fails when valid request success rate falls below 99%
preconditions: Performance test environment can simulate degraded conditions (service errors, DB contention, or capacity limits) affecting rule-pass request processing.
Load script records per-request classification (rule-pass vs rule-fail) and terminal processing outcome.
Release gate documentation requires explicit pass/fail evidence when NFR-03 is not met.
steps: Run pilot load with rule-pass requests under controlled degradation until observed success rate approaches or falls below 99%.
Capture metric snapshot: total rule-pass requests, processing successes, processing failures (5xx/timeout/unpersisted), and computed rate.
Compare rate against NFR-03 >= 99% threshold and document gate pass/fail verdict.
Run control scenario under normal pilot profile to confirm PASS boundary when rate >= 99%.
cases.expected: When computed valid-request success rate is < 99%, NFR-03 gate is marked FAIL with documented metric evidence; when rate >= 99% under normal pilot profile, gate is PASS. Boundary validates measurability of the 99% non-functional threshold.

cases.title: Non-Student roles denied POST /v1/check-ins and excluded from valid success-rate denominator
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Lecturer and ITAdmin bearer tokens are available per permission matrix (Submit QR check-in Deny).
Enrolled Student bearer token is available as authorized control for success-rate baseline.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken and Authorization header.
ITAdmin calls POST /v1/check-ins with the same qrToken context.
Verify both receive HTTP 403 Forbidden per permission matrix.
Run parallel rule-pass Student submissions; compute success rate using only authenticated enrolled Student rule-pass requests.
cases.expected: Lecturer and ITAdmin receive 403 Forbidden; no CheckInAttempt or AttendanceRecord is created for non-student actors. Unauthorized attempts are excluded from NFR-03 valid-request denominator; Student rule-pass success rate remains >= 99%.

cases.title: FLOW-01 mobile-web check-in journeys achieve >= 99% UI success confirmation under pilot load
preconditions: Multiple open sessions with enrolled students are configured for parallel browser journeys.
Student actors use mobile-web viewport (Chromium mobile emulation) with valid check-in deep links.
Browser harness classifies each journey as rule-pass (eligible) and records terminal UI state (success-present, success-late, or processing failure).
steps: Navigate enrolled students in parallel to /check-in?token=... with valid tokens.
Complete login redirect if required; grant GPS prompt when policy requires.
Submit check-in and wait for CheckInResultScreen success status or error state.
Aggregate UI processing success rate for rule-pass journeys only across the pilot cohort.
cases.expected: >= 99% of rule-pass mobile-web check-in journeys reach CheckInResultScreen with success-present or success-late confirmation, satisfying NFR-03 processing success gate for UI-facing student flow.

cases.title: Pilot campus valid check-in success rate on physical mobile browsers under class-start load
preconditions: Staging pilot uses real iOS Safari or Android Chrome on campus Wi-Fi per testing-plan §6.2 browser matrix.
Multiple open class sessions with enrolled students; lecturer displays classroom QR.
Physical devices and campus network peak load are not reproducible in Playwright MCP harness.
steps: During representative class-start window, enrolled students scan QR and complete check-in on physical mobile devices.
Classify each attempt as rule-pass or rule-fail; record whether processing completed with success confirmation.
Aggregate valid-request success rate across pilot sessions.
Compare against NFR-03 >= 99% threshold for release evidence.
cases.expected: Physical pilot demonstrates >= 99% processing success for rule-pass check-in requests under real campus load, providing production-like evidence for NFR-03 beyond emulated harness runs.
